5	**Governs**: [ADR-014](./adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md), [CONTRACT-004](./contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md)
6	**Supersedes scope of**: ADR-013 (withdrawn pending this refactor)
7	**Rough size**: 12 numbered steps totaling ~20–25 PRs over 6–10 weeks for one engineer serialized (less with parallelism across the per-harness migrations after Step 4 lands); Steps 5/6/7 each land as 4–6 sub-PRs
8	
9	## Problem Statement
10	
11	The service depends on roughly 80 exported symbols across the per-harness
12	packages beyond the documented `Harness` interface — quota cache I/O,
13	routing-decision functions, concrete snapshot types with field-level
14	reads, family-alias resolvers, PTY probe entry points, and account-file
15	readers. A 2026-05-14 inventory counted **69 external call sites** across
16	six service files. The `Harness` interface alone does not capture the
17	contract; adding any new harness today requires either duplicating ~25
18	symbols under a new prefix or rewiring service code per-harness.
19	
20	CONTRACT-004 defines the universal interface. This plan ports every
21	existing harness, every service consumer, every test, and the
22	public-facing CONTRACT-003 projection onto that contract, then enforces
23	the boundary with a lint rule.
24	
25	## Requirements
26	
27	### Functional
28	
29	- Every existing harness implements the CONTRACT-004 interfaces it
30	  qualifies for: `Harness` (all), `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex,
31	  gemini), `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex),
32	  `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five).
33	- Service-side code reads quota, routing preference, account state,
34	  and model discovery exclusively through the interfaces. No file in
35	  `service*.go`, `internal/serviceimpl/`, `internal/runtimesignals/`,
36	  or `cmd/` imports per-harness package symbols beyond the runner
37	  constructor used by the dispatcher.
38	- Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot`, `*QuotaRoutingDecision`,
39	  `*QuotaCachePath`, `Read*Quota*`, `Write*Quota*`,
40	  `Default*QuotaStaleAfter`, `Decide*QuotaRouting`,
41	  `Default*ModelDiscovery`, and `Resolve*Alias` symbols are removed
42	  from the public surface (deleted or unexported).
43	- Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes (`HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
44	  `QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`) remain structurally identical (same
45	  field set, same value semantics) to pre-refactor recorded fixtures.
46	- The service has a single async refresh scheduler that consumes
47	  `QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`
48	  to drive refreshes, replacing per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions.
49	
50	### Non-Functional
51	
52	- Refactor is staged one harness at a time so regressions surface
53	  early.
54	- The lint rule that enforces the boundary lands in the last step;
55	  partial migrations cannot accidentally satisfy it.
56	- No record-mode cassette is invalidated; cache file on-disk JSON
57	  shapes remain stable (cache file schemas use field-tagged JSON,
58	  not type-name encoding).
59	- Pre-existing test suite runtime does not regress by more than 10%
60	  (measured as `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time before
61	  Step 0 and after the final step, excluding new test packages
62	  introduced by Steps 2 and 4). No individual pre-existing test slows
63	  by more than 10%. New conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted
64	  and reviewed separately in the Step 11 PR.
65

140	## Implementation Steps
141	
142	Sequenced so the contract is testable before any migration, projection
143	helpers and the conformance suite exist before the first harness lands,
144	and the lint rule lands last. Each step is one or more PRs as noted;
145	the per-harness migration steps (Step 5–8) are explicitly multi-PR
146	sub-sequences.
147	
148	### Step 0 — Pre-refactor baseline
149	
150	**Goal**: Pin the surfaces that must not regress.
151	
152	**Work**:
153	- Add `service_contract_snapshot_test.go` fixtures (or extend the
154	  existing one) serializing `HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
155	  `QuotaState`, and `AccountStatus` to JSON under a deterministic
156	  scenario set: one of each of the five harnesses, plus a
157	  representative provider with quota. Fixtures pinned under
158	  `testdata/contract-003/pre-refactor/`.
159	- Run `grep -rn "internal/harnesses/\(claude\|codex\|gemini\|opencode\|pi\)" --include="*.go" .`
160	  over the entire repository, excluding the harness packages themselves,
161	  `node_modules/`, `.claude/worktrees/`, `benchmark-results/`, and
162	  `.ddx/`. List the full result set in the Step 0 PR description.
163	  Add any file not already in the "Service-side consumers" or "Tests"
164	  inventory tables above. (Current expectation: zero unknowns — CLI
165	  binaries route through `Service.*` — but verify before any
166	  migration step starts.)
167	- Record `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time as the
168	  pre-refactor baseline. Save in the PR description and in
169	  `testdata/perf-baselines/2026-05-14-pre-refactor.txt`. This is the
170	  baseline that Step 11's regression budget compares against. Note
171	  that Step 2 (conformance suite) and Step 4 (scheduler) add genuine
172	  new test coverage; Step 11's budget accounts for them separately
173	  rather than asserting a flat 10% cap on total runtime.
174	
175	**Verification of refactor**: Step 11 re-runs the fixtures with a
176	structural JSON diff (not byte-equal) that ignores `CapturedAt`
177	formatting variance and any documented additive fields, asserting
178	all other shapes are preserved.
179	
180	**Size**: 1 PR.
181	
182	### Step 1 — Add interfaces and universal types
183	
184	**Goal**: CONTRACT-004 declarations exist; no harness implements them yet.
185	
186	**Work**:
187	- Edit `internal/harnesses/types.go`:
188	  - Add `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, `ModelDiscoveryHarness`
189	    interface declarations.
190	  - Add `QuotaStatus`, `AccountSnapshot`, `QuotaStateValue`,
191	    `RoutingPreference` type declarations.
192	  - Add `ErrAliasNotResolvable` sentinel error.
193	- Add unit tests covering type defaults, RoutingPreference enum
194	  identity, and that `QuotaStateValue` string conversion matches the
195	  documented CONTRACT-004 values.
196	
197	**Size**: 1 PR.
198	
199	### Step 2 — Conformance suite and synthetic harness
200	
201	**Goal**: The contract is testable. Every later migration step runs
202	against this suite.
203	
204	**Work**:
205	- New package `internal/harnesses/harnesstest/` (test helper, not
206	  test-only) containing:
207	  - `RunHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.Harness)` — asserts
208	    `Info`, `HealthCheck` (without invoking the binary), and the
209	    `Execute` setup-vs-runtime-error contract.
210	  - `RunQuotaHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.QuotaHarness)` —
211	    asserts `QuotaStatus` returns a valid `QuotaStatus` value (not
212	    an error) when the cache is empty, asserts `QuotaFreshness` is
213	    positive, reads `SupportedLimitIDs()` and asserts every emitted
214	    `Windows[].LimitID` (after driving a `RefreshQuota` against a
215	    cassette or synthetic state) is a member of that set. The suite
216	    asserts the **contract shape** of single-flight semantics —
217	    that concurrent `RefreshQuota` calls return without error and
218	    share a consistent post-state — but does not assert "exactly one
219	    probe ran." Probe-count single-flight is testable on the
220	    synthetic harness (in-memory counter; covered in the synthetic
221	    test); on real harnesses (claude/codex/gemini) it requires
222	    binary-specific or filesystem-specific harnessing and lives in
223	    each harness's own package tests, not the shared suite.
224	  - `RunAccountHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.AccountHarness)` —
225	    analogous, including `AccountFreshness`.
226	  - `RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.ModelDiscoveryHarness)` —
227	    asserts non-empty `DefaultModelSnapshot`, reads
228	    `SupportedAliases()`, asserts `ResolveModelAlias` resolves each
229	    listed alias (positive path) and returns
230	    `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for an out-of-set family (negative
231	    path). Empty `SupportedAliases()` skips the positive-path
232	    check (opencode/pi case).
233	  - `NewSyntheticQuotaHarness(name string, status QuotaStatus, limitIDs []string) QuotaHarness`,
234	    `NewSyntheticAccountHarness(name string, snapshot AccountSnapshot, freshness time.Duration) AccountHarness`,
235	    `NewSyntheticModelDiscoveryHarness(name string, snapshot ModelDiscoverySnapshot, aliases []string) ModelDiscoveryHarness` —
236	    in-memory implementations used by service-level tests to replace
237	    per-harness snapshot construction. Constructors accept the values
238	    each `Supported*` method should return so tests can exercise both
239	    populated and empty contract surfaces.
240	- Add a smoke test that runs the synthetic harnesses against the
241	  conformance suite (proves the suite works before any real harness
242	  consumes it).
243	
244	**Size**: 1 PR.

247	
248	**Goal**: One projection helper per public type. The first harness
249	migration consumes them.

252	- New file `service_projection.go` (or extend existing) with:
253	  - `func projectQuotaStatus(qs harnesses.QuotaStatus) *QuotaState`
254	  - `func projectAccountSnapshot(as harnesses.AccountSnapshot) *AccountStatus`
255	- Unit tests drive the helpers with synthetic
256	  `QuotaStatus`/`AccountSnapshot` values covering every
257	  `QuotaStateValue`, every `RoutingPreference`, populated and empty

263	**Size**: 1 PR.

265	### Step 4 — Async refresh scheduler
266	
267	**Goal**: A single service-level scheduler consumes
268	`QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`
269	to drive refreshes. Replaces today's per-harness
270	`Refresh*Async`/`refreshClaudeQuotaAsync` helpers.
271	
272	**Work**:
273	- **Introduce a `harnessByName(name string) harnesses.Harness`
274	  method on the service struct** (consulting the registered-harnesses
275	  field) if it does not already exist. This is the seam every later
276	  step uses to fetch the registered harness instance and type-assert
277	  it to the optional sub-interfaces. Logically belongs with the
278	  scheduler; lifting it here means Step 5b can consume it without
279	  introducing it.
280	- New file `service_refresh_scheduler.go`. Owns:
281	  - A debounced ticker per registered `QuotaHarness` keyed by
282	    harness name, firing at `QuotaFreshness()/2` and calling
283	    `RefreshQuota(ctx)`.
284	  - Analogous tickers for `AccountHarness` instances whose
285	    freshness window differs from quota's (e.g. gemini's 7-day
286	    auth).
287	  - Startup refresh kickoff (cache-cold path from
288	    `primary-harness-capability-baseline.md`).
289	  - Tests with a fake clock that prove the scheduler debounces,
290	    respects single-flight (because `RefreshQuota` blocks on the
291	    harness's cache lock), and exits cleanly on context cancel.
292	- **Harness registration mechanism**: at construction time the
293	  scheduler iterates `service.harnessByName(name)` over every name
294	  in the existing harness registry and type-asserts each result to
295	  `QuotaHarness` and `AccountHarness` independently. Pre-migration,
296	  type assertions fail for non-migrated harnesses and the scheduler
297	  registers nothing for them — they continue to use their existing
298	  `Refresh*Async` helpers. Post-migration (Steps 5–8), the
299	  assertions succeed and the migrated harness participates. No
300	  explicit per-harness registration call is needed; the scheduler
301	  picks up new harnesses on its next construction.
302	- The scheduler is the only place that deduplicates probe calls by
303	  cache freshness; `QuotaHarness.RefreshQuota` itself probes
304	  unconditionally per CONTRACT-004. The scheduler skips a tick when
305	  the last-known `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` is within
306	  `QuotaFreshness()` of `now`.
307	- Per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions are not yet deleted — they
308	  stay until their owning harness migrates in Steps 5–8. Each
309	  harness's Step 5e/6e/7e sub-PR deletes its `Refresh*Async` after
310	  confirming the scheduler is driving its refresh cadence.
311	
312	**Size**: 1 PR.

374	5a–5d) leaves the tree compiling and tests green: the consumers in

376	not care whether the snapshot type is exported. This is the

491	
492	**Goal**: Confirm public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes are structurally
493	identical to Step 0 captures.
494	
495	**Work**:
496	- Re-run the Step 0 fixtures with structural diff. Implementation: a
497	  small test helper at `internal/test/structuraldiff/diff.go` (or
498	  equivalent location the Go toolchain compiles — NOT under
499	  `testdata/`, which is excluded from builds) that unmarshals both
500	  fixtures into `map[string]any`, walks the tree, and applies
501	  documented field-level ignores (RFC3339-shape check for
502	  `CapturedAt` and other `time.Time` fields; presence-only for any
503	  field flagged opaque in CONTRACT-004).
504	  - Field set must be identical (no fields added or removed unless
505	    intentional and documented).
506	  - Field values must match semantically (string equality except
507	    `time.Time` fields, which are compared under the "valid RFC3339,

512	  - Pre-existing test suite (everything outside the new
513	    `internal/harnesses/harnesstest` and `service_refresh_scheduler*`
514	    paths) within 10% of Step 0 baseline.
515	  - No individual pre-existing test slows by more than 10%
516	    (measured with `-count=1` and the `-v` flag plus a
517	    timing-extraction script).
518	  - The new conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted separately
519	    in the Step 11 PR description (they did not exist at Step 0 so
520	    a percentage delta is meaningless; instead the absolute new
521	    runtime is reported and reviewed).

523	**Acceptance**: Public JSON shapes match Step 0 fixtures structurally;
524	test runtime within budget.

529	
530	**Goal**: Spec and code-doc consistency.
531	
532	**Work**:
533	- Update PRD line 204 ("implement the harness interface
534	  (CONTRACT-003)") to cite CONTRACT-004.
535	- Update `primary-harness-capability-baseline.md` if any capability
536	  evidence pointer cites a deleted symbol.
537	- Update CONTRACT-003 to cross-reference CONTRACT-004 as the source
538	  for `HarnessInfo.Quota` and `HarnessInfo.Account` population.
539	- Update AGENTS.md (or equivalent contributor guide) to describe the
540	  harness implementation pattern: implement `Harness` plus optional
541	  sub-interfaces, keep snapshot types package-private, no cross-harness
542	  imports, expose stable contract surfaces via `SupportedAliases()`
543	  and `SupportedLimitIDs()`.
544	- Verify each `internal/harnesses/<name>/` package has a doc comment
545	  enumerating its `SupportedLimitIDs()` (where applicable) and
546	  `SupportedAliases()` return values, matching the programmatic
547	  source of truth. A Go test using `go/doc` parses each harness's
548	  package comment, extracts the documented sets, and asserts equality
549	  with the values the `Supported*` methods return on a default
550	  `Runner{}` instance. Drift fails CI.

553	deleted symbol; every harness package has a doc-comment mirror of
554	its `Supported*` exports.

556	**Size**: 1 PR.
557	
558	## Test Strategy
559	
560	| Layer | Coverage |
561	|-------|----------|
562	| Unit (per harness) | Interface method implementations; routing-preference mapping; cache I/O against synthetic files. |
563	| Conformance suite | Every harness runs Step 2 assertions for its declared interfaces. Suite tests itself against the synthetic harness in Step 2. |
564	| Service-level | JSON-shape preservation against Step 0 fixtures via structural diff in Step 11; routing decisions against synthetic `QuotaHarness` implementations covering every `RoutingPreference` × `QuotaStateValue` combination. |
565	| Integration | Existing `harness_golden_integration_test.go` cassettes continue to pass without modification (cassettes are below the interface boundary). |
566	| Concurrency | The conformance suite asserts `RefreshQuota` and `RefreshAccount` single-flight under concurrent invocation (CONTRACT-004 invariant #7). |
567	| Lint | Step 10 boundary rule runs in CI. |
568	| Regression | Pre-existing test suite within 10% of Step 0 baseline (asserted in Step 11); no individual pre-existing test slower by more than 10%; new conformance + scheduler tests budgeted separately. |
569	
570	## Rollback Strategy
571	
572	Each step's PR is independently revertable.
573	
574	- **Cache file compatibility**: All per-harness cache files use
575	  field-tagged JSON encoding. Renaming the Go type from
576	  `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` to `claudeQuotaSnapshot` does not change the
577	  on-disk JSON shape (verified for each harness in Step 5a/6a/7a
578	  before unexporting). A revert of Step 5e reintroduces the exported
579	  type name without invalidating cached files written under the new
580	  code.
581	- **Per-harness rollback**: Reverting Step 5 (claude) does not require
582	  reverting Steps 6–8 (codex/gemini/opencode/pi) because each
583	  harness's interface implementation is independent. The reverted
584	  harness goes back to its per-harness public surface; the others
585	  remain on the interface.
586	- **Pre-lint coexistence**: Step 10's lint rule lands last
587	  intentionally so that partial-state environments (some harnesses
588	  migrated, some not) compile and test. A revert during this window
589	  produces a working tree; a revert after Step 10 may require
590	  reverting Step 10 alongside.
591	- **Public JSON shape**: Step 11's structural-diff assertion is the
592	  guarantee that any merged state preserves CONTRACT-003. A diff at
593	  Step 11 blocks merge; rollback is a revert of the offending step,
594	  not a forward-fix on a broken contract.
595	
596	## Risks and Mitigations
597	
598	| Risk | Mitigation |
599	|------|------------|
600	| JSON shape regression during projection refactor | Step 0 fixtures pinned before any refactor work; Step 11 asserts structural equality with documented time-field handling. |
601	| Routing semantics drift (boolean `PreferX` → `RoutingPreference` enum) | Step 5 (claude) lands fully and runs the routing test suite before Step 6 (codex) begins. Each subsequent harness re-runs the suite. |
602	| Cache file schema drift when snapshots are renamed | Cache files use field-tagged JSON; type renames don't affect on-disk shape. Each Step 5a/6a/7a includes a load-old-write-new round-trip test before unexporting. |
603	| Conformance suite is too strict and blocks legitimate variation | Conformance covers only CONTRACT-004 contract behavior. Harness-specific tests stay in the harness package. The suite is itself tested in Step 2 against the synthetic harness. |
604	| Lint exemption file accumulates as a backdoor | The exemption list in Step 10 is one line; growing it requires an ADR amendment. |
605	| Refactor stalls partway, leaving mixed surface | Step 10 (lint rule) lands last; partial state is a valid intermediate. Each harness migration's sub-sequence (5e/6e/7e/8) deletes the old surface as an explicit acceptance gate before the next harness starts. |
606	| `claude-tui` work resurfaces during the refactor and bypasses it | ADR-013 is withdrawn (not paused); re-proposal must cite the merged CONTRACT-004. |
607	| Async refresh scheduler is wrong and refreshes too often or too rarely | Step 4's scheduler tests use a fake clock and assert exact tick counts per `QuotaFreshness()` value. Production scheduling defects are caught by `service_status` integration tests measuring actual refresh cadence. |
608	| Step 5 sub-sequence stalls partway (5b lands, 5c does not) | Intermediate states (5a-5d) are valid: claude's new interface methods coexist with the old surface. The old surface only disappears in 5e. |
609	
610	## Acceptance Criteria
611	
612	1. `internal/harnesses/types.go` declares the four interfaces and
613	   universal types per CONTRACT-004.
614	2. Every existing harness implements the interfaces appropriate to it
615	   (verified by the Step 2 conformance suite).
616	3. No `.go` file outside `internal/harnesses/` imports a symbol from
617	   `internal/harnesses/<name>/` except
618	   `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`.
619	4. Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot` types are lowercase.
620	5. Per-harness `*QuotaRoutingDecision` types are deleted.
621	6. Per-harness cache I/O functions are unexported.
622	7. The async refresh scheduler from Step 4 owns all per-harness
623	   refresh cadence; per-harness `Refresh*Async` helpers are deleted.
624	8. CONTRACT-003 JSON fixtures (Step 0) pass structural diff
625	   post-refactor (Step 11).
626	9. Lint rule from Step 10 runs in CI and blocks new forbidden imports.
627	10. PRD, CONTRACT-003, and AGENTS.md cross-references are updated.
628	11. Pre-existing test suite runtime is within 10% of the Step 0
629	    baseline (excluding new packages from Steps 2 and 4); no
630	    individual pre-existing test slows by more than 10%.
631	
632	## Follow-Ups (Out of Scope)
633	
634	- **`claude-tui` re-proposal.** Once this refactor lands, ADR-013
635	  may be re-proposed citing CONTRACT-004. The fork then becomes
636	  additive: a new `internal/harnesses/claude-tui/` package
637	  implementing the same four interfaces, sharing a snapshot type with
638	  `claude` through an `internal/harnesses/anthropic/` neutral
639	  subpackage (since both snapshots are now package-private, sharing
640	  them requires only moving the type into a shared internal
641	  package).
642	
643	  ADR-013's withdrawal note records design constraints for the
644	  re-proposal — prior art surveyed (claude-p, shannon),
645	  transcript-JSONL-as-evidence convergence, hooks-driven transport
646	  via `--settings '<inline-json>'`, pooled long-lived sessions with
647	  `/clear` between turns bounded by the fiz process lifetime, pool
648	  key per `(harness, workdir)` at package or service scope (not on
649	  the Runner struct), startup orphan reaper analogous to the
650	  existing `service_stale_harness_reaper*.go`, hook-conflict
651	  handling against operator `~/.claude/settings.json`, and the
652	  pre-implementation gate that verifies `/clear` semantics against
653	  the installed Claude Code version. Re-proposal still requires
654	  empirical evidence that PTY+hooks-driven Claude moves the
655	  `/usage` window — the structural refactor here does not validate
656	  that premise. CONTRACT-004 and ADR-014 are transport-agnostic and
657	  unaffected by these constraints; they live below the interface.
658	- **`HarnessConfig` registry cleanup.** The current `HarnessConfig`
659	  struct mixes interface-relevant metadata with subprocess-specific
660	  knobs. Splitting these is a separate cleanup; CONTRACT-004 does
661	  not require it.
662	- **Provider-side interface.** HTTP providers (`openrouter`,
663	  `lmstudio`, `omlx`) have their own per-provider exports. A
664	  parallel `ProviderHarness` contract is plausible but not in scope.
665

data.output:
1	# Implementation Plan: Universal Harness Interface Refactor
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-14
4	**Status**: DRAFT
5	**Governs**: [ADR-014](./adr/ADR-014-universal-harness-interface.md), [CONTRACT-004](./contracts/CONTRACT-004-harness-implementation.md)
6	**Supersedes scope of**: ADR-013 (withdrawn pending this refactor)
7	**Rough size**: 12 numbered steps totaling ~20–25 PRs over 6–10 weeks for one engineer serialized (less with parallelism across the per-harness migrations after Step 4 lands); Steps 5/6/7 each land as 4–6 sub-PRs
8	
9	## Problem Statement
10	
11	The service depends on roughly 80 exported symbols across the per-harness
12	packages beyond the documented `Harness` interface — quota cache I/O,
13	routing-decision functions, concrete snapshot types with field-level
14	reads, family-alias resolvers, PTY probe entry points, and account-file
15	readers. A 2026-05-14 inventory counted **69 external call sites** across
16	six service files. The `Harness` interface alone does not capture the
17	contract; adding any new harness today requires either duplicating ~25
18	symbols under a new prefix or rewiring service code per-harness.
19	
20	CONTRACT-004 defines the universal interface. This plan ports every
21	existing harness, every service consumer, every test, and the
22	public-facing CONTRACT-003 projection onto that contract, then enforces
23	the boundary with a lint rule.
24	
25	## Requirements
26	
27	### Functional
28	
29	- Every existing harness implements the CONTRACT-004 interfaces it
30	  qualifies for: `Harness` (all), `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex,
31	  gemini), `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex),
32	  `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five).
33	- Service-side code reads quota, routing preference, account state,
34	  and model discovery exclusively through the interfaces. No file in
35	  `service*.go`, `internal/serviceimpl/`, `internal/runtimesignals/`,
36	  or `cmd/` imports per-harness package symbols beyond the runner
37	  constructor used by the dispatcher.
38	- Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot`, `*QuotaRoutingDecision`,
39	  `*QuotaCachePath`, `Read*Quota*`, `Write*Quota*`,
40	  `Default*QuotaStaleAfter`, `Decide*QuotaRouting`,
41	  `Default*ModelDiscovery`, and `Resolve*Alias` symbols are removed
42	  from the public surface (deleted or unexported).
43	- Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes (`HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
44	  `QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`) remain structurally identical (same
45	  field set, same value semantics) to pre-refactor recorded fixtures.
46	- The service has a single async refresh scheduler that consumes
47	  `QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`
48	  to drive refreshes, replacing per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions.
49	
50	### Non-Functional
51	
52	- Refactor is staged one harness at a time so regressions surface
53	  early.
54	- The lint rule that enforces the boundary lands in the last step;
55	  partial migrations cannot accidentally satisfy it.
56	- No record-mode cassette is invalidated; cache file on-disk JSON
57	  shapes remain stable (cache file schemas use field-tagged JSON,
58	  not type-name encoding).
59	- Pre-existing test suite runtime does not regress by more than 10%
60	  (measured as `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time before
61	  Step 0 and after the final step, excluding new test packages
62	  introduced by Steps 2 and 4). No individual pre-existing test slows
63	  by more than 10%. New conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted
64	  and reviewed separately in the Step 11 PR.
65	
66	## Touch-Point Inventory
67	
68	Every file that changes, with the symbols being migrated.
69	
70	### Harness packages
71	
72	| Package | Changes |
73	|---------|---------|
74	| `internal/harnesses/types.go` | Add `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, `ModelDiscoveryHarness` interfaces; add `QuotaStatus`, `AccountSnapshot`, `QuotaStateValue`, `RoutingPreference`, `ErrAliasNotResolvable` sentinel. Keep existing `Harness` interface and existing public types untouched. |
75	| `internal/harnesses/claude/quota_cache.go` | Lowercase `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` → `claudeQuotaSnapshot`. Unexport `ReadClaudeQuota`, `ReadClaudeQuotaFrom`, `WriteClaudeQuota`, `ClaudeQuotaCachePath`, `IsClaudeQuotaFresh`, `ClaudeQuotaSnapshotAge`, `DefaultClaudeQuotaStaleAfter`. Delete `ClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision`, `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting`, `ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision`. |
76	| `internal/harnesses/claude/runner.go` | Add `QuotaStatus`, `RefreshQuota`, `QuotaFreshness`, `AccountStatus`, `RefreshAccount`, `AccountFreshness`, `DefaultModelSnapshot`, `ResolveModelAlias` methods on `*Runner`. Internally call the now-unexported cache I/O. Acquire the harness's cache lock around refresh. |
77	| `internal/harnesses/claude/quota_pty.go` | Unexport `ReadClaudeQuotaViaPTY`, `RefreshClaudeQuotaViaPTY`, `ReadClaudeQuotaFromCassette`. Become internal helpers. |
78	| `internal/harnesses/claude/model_discovery.go` | Unexport `DefaultClaudeModelDiscovery`, `ResolveClaudeFamilyAlias`, `ReadClaudeReasoningFromHelp`. |
79	| `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go` | Mirror of claude: lowercase snapshot, unexport cache I/O, delete decision struct/func. |
80	| `internal/harnesses/codex/runner.go` | Add interface methods. Codex's session-token-count quota source folds into `RefreshQuota` as an internal fallback. |
81	| `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_pty.go` | Unexport. |
82	| `internal/harnesses/codex/session_token_count.go` | Unexport `ReadCodexQuotaFromSessionTokenCounts`, `CodexQuotaSnapshotFromTokenCountRateLimits`, `CodexSessionsRoot`. |
83	| `internal/harnesses/codex/account.go` | Unexport `ReadCodexAccount`, `ReadCodexAccountFrom`, `CodexAuthPath`. Used internally from `AccountStatus`. |
84	| `internal/harnesses/codex/model_discovery.go` | Unexport `DefaultCodexModelDiscovery`, `ResolveCodexModelAlias`. |
85	| `internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_cache.go` | Mirror. Tier-specific facts go into `QuotaStatus.Windows` (one per tier with `LimitID`); `Detail` carries only free-form diagnostic notes. |
86	| `internal/harnesses/gemini/runner.go` | Add interface methods. Gemini implements `AccountHarness` explicitly with `AccountFreshness()` returning 7 days. |
87	| `internal/harnesses/gemini/auth.go` | Unexport `ReadAuthEvidence`, `ReadAuthEvidenceFromDir`, `AuthSnapshot`, `GeminiAuthFreshnessWindow`. Used internally from `AccountStatus`. |
88	| `internal/harnesses/gemini/model_discovery.go` | Unexport `DefaultGeminiModelDiscovery`, `ResolveGeminiModelAlias`. |
89	| `internal/harnesses/opencode/runner.go` | Add `DefaultModelSnapshot`, `ResolveModelAlias` (returns `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for unknown families). Does not implement `QuotaHarness` or `AccountHarness`. |
90	| `internal/harnesses/opencode/model_discovery.go` | Unexport `DefaultOpenCodeModelDiscovery`. Decide fate of `OpenCodeModelEvidence`/`OpenCodeModelCost` in Step 8. |
91	| `internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go` | Add `DefaultModelSnapshot`, `ResolveModelAlias`. |
92	| `internal/harnesses/pi/model_discovery.go` | Unexport `DefaultPiModelDiscovery`. |
93	
94	### Service-side consumers
95	
96	The complete list of files that import per-harness packages today
97	and the symbols each uses.
98	
99	| File | Current imports | Symbols used | Migration |
100	|------|-----------------|--------------|-----------|
101	| `service.go:12-14` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness`, `geminiharness` | `ReadClaudeQuota` (×2), `ClaudeQuotaCachePath` (×1), `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting` (×2), `ReadCodexQuota` (×2), `CodexQuotaCachePath` (×1), `IsCodexQuotaFresh` (×1), `DecideCodexQuotaRouting` (×1), `ReadGeminiQuota` (×1), `GeminiQuotaCachePath` (×1), `IsGeminiQuotaFresh` (×1), `ReadAuthEvidence` (×1) | Drop the three `*harness` imports. Replace each call site with `qh, ok := h.(harnesses.QuotaHarness); if ok { qs, _ := qh.QuotaStatus(ctx, now) }` etc., fetching `h` via the `harnessByName` helper introduced in Step 4. Both registry patterns (same-instance-per-call vs construct-fresh) work under CONTRACT-004 invariant #6. |
102	| `service_providers.go:22-23` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness` | `ReadClaudeQuotaViaPTY`, `ClaudeQuotaCachePath`, `ReadClaudeQuotaFrom`, `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting`, `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot{}` (constructor), `WriteClaudeQuota` (×2), codex equivalents | All cache I/O and snapshot construction moves behind `QuotaHarness.RefreshQuota`. Health-check callbacks at lines 37, 41-45 become `qh.RefreshQuota(ctx)` calls. |
103	| `service_models.go:19-21` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness`, `geminiharness` | `DefaultClaudeModelDiscovery`, `ResolveClaudeFamilyAlias`, codex/gemini equivalents | Replace with `mdh, ok := h.(harnesses.ModelDiscoveryHarness); if ok { snap := mdh.DefaultModelSnapshot(); model, err := mdh.ResolveModelAlias(family, snap) }`. |
104	| `service_subscription_quota.go:7-9` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness`, `geminiharness` | `ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision`, `ReadCodexQuotaRoutingDecision`, `ReadGeminiQuotaRoutingDecision`, type assertion `*claudeharness.ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` at line 79 | Replace decision reads with `QuotaStatus.RoutingPreference`. The `claudeQuotaMaxUsedPercent` helper at line 79 currently computes max-used over snapshot fields (`FiveHourRemaining`, `WeeklyRemaining`); under the refactor this becomes derivation over `QuotaStatus.Windows[i].UsedPercent`, which both claude and codex expose uniformly. Decided in Step 4 (claude migration) — not deferred. |
105	| `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go:9-13` | All five `*harness` packages | `&claudeharness.Runner{}`, `&codexharness.Runner{}`, etc. (constructor only) | **Retained.** This is the explicit runner-constructor seam allowed by CONTRACT-004. The dispatcher continues to know concrete runner types. |
106	| `internal/runtimesignals/collect.go:11-13` | `claudecache`, `codexcache`, `geminicache` | Per-harness cache reads for runtime-signal collection | Replace with `qh, ok := h.(harnesses.QuotaHarness); if ok { qs, _ := qh.QuotaStatus(ctx, now); ... }`. |
107	
108	### Tests that consume per-harness packages
109	
110	| File | Current usage | Migration |
111	|------|---------------|-----------|
112	| `service_status_test.go` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness` snapshot constructors | Synthetic `QuotaHarness` implementations (Step 2's conformance suite ships a `synthQuotaHarness` helper). |
113	| `service_route_attempts_test.go` | `codexharness`, `geminiharness` | Synthetic `QuotaHarness` implementations. |
114	| `harness_golden_integration_test.go` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness` | Cassette-driven; cassettes unaffected. Test seeds become interface-level. |
115	| `service_subscription_quota_test.go` | `claudeharness` snapshots | Synthetic `QuotaHarness`. |
116	| `service_routing_errors_test.go` | `claudeharness` | Synthetic `QuotaHarness`. |
117	| `service_providers_test.go` | `claudeharness`, `codexharness` | Synthetic `QuotaHarness` plus fixture cache writes for refresh tests. |
118	| `service_routing_test.go` | `claudeharness` | Synthetic `QuotaHarness`. |
119	| `service_execute_dispatch_test.go` | All five package strings (lines 45-49) | No change — test asserts dispatcher recognizes harness names; package paths remain. |
120	| `internal/harnesses/runner_info_parity_test.go` | `claudeharness` | Moves into Step 2's conformance suite as a shared assertion. |
121	| `internal/runtimesignals/collect_test.go` | `claudecache` | Synthetic `QuotaHarness` or fixture cache. |
122	| `internal/harnesses/<name>/*_test.go` | Currently mixed `package <name>` and `package <name>_test` | All harness-local tests run as `package <name>` (white-box) so they access unexported helpers after the refactor. Black-box tests that exist today are reviewed and either flipped to white-box or rewritten against the public interface. |
123	
124	### Embedding-interface projection sites
125	
126	| Public field (CONTRACT-003) | Defined at | Current source | Refactor source |
127	|------------------------------|------------|----------------|-----------------|
128	| `service.QuotaState.Windows` | `service.go:167` | Per-harness snapshot's `Windows` field | `QuotaStatus.Windows` |
129	| `service.QuotaState.CapturedAt` | `service.go:168` | Per-harness snapshot's `CapturedAt` | `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` |
130	| `service.QuotaState.Fresh` | `service.go:169` | Per-harness `Is*QuotaFresh` | `QuotaStatus.Fresh` |
131	| `service.QuotaState.Source` | `service.go:170` | Per-harness snapshot's `Source` | `QuotaStatus.Source` |
132	| `service.QuotaState.Status` | `service.go:171` | Computed by service from snapshot windows | `string(QuotaStatus.State)` with ` (<Reason>)` suffix when non-empty (per CONTRACT-004 projection) |
133	| `service.QuotaState.LastError` | `service.go:172` | Set by service when probe error surfaces | Non-nil only on interface-method call failure |
134	| `service.AccountStatus.*` | `service.go:186-196` | Per-harness account read | `AccountSnapshot.*` for harnesses implementing `AccountHarness` |
135	| `service.HarnessInfo.Quota` | `service.go:232+` | Built per harness name | Single helper consuming `QuotaStatus` |
136	| `service.HarnessInfo.Account` | `service.go:232+` | Built per harness name | Single helper consuming `AccountSnapshot` |
137	| `service.ProviderInfo.Quota` | `service.go` ProviderInfo assembly | Same | Same |
138	| `service.ProviderInfo.Auth` | Same | Same | Same |
139	
140	## Implementation Steps
141	
142	Sequenced so the contract is testable before any migration, projection
143	helpers and the conformance suite exist before the first harness lands,
144	and the lint rule lands last. Each step is one or more PRs as noted;
145	the per-harness migration steps (Step 5–8) are explicitly multi-PR
146	sub-sequences.
147	
148	### Step 0 — Pre-refactor baseline
149	
150	**Goal**: Pin the surfaces that must not regress.
151	
152	**Work**:
153	- Add `service_contract_snapshot_test.go` fixtures (or extend the
154	  existing one) serializing `HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
155	  `QuotaState`, and `AccountStatus` to JSON under a deterministic
156	  scenario set: one of each of the five harnesses, plus a
157	  representative provider with quota. Fixtures pinned under
158	  `testdata/contract-003/pre-refactor/`.
159	- Run `grep -rn "internal/harnesses/\(claude\|codex\|gemini\|opencode\|pi\)" --include="*.go" .`
160	  over the entire repository, excluding the harness packages themselves,
161	  `node_modules/`, `.claude/worktrees/`, `benchmark-results/`, and
162	  `.ddx/`. List the full result set in the Step 0 PR description.
163	  Add any file not already in the "Service-side consumers" or "Tests"
164	  inventory tables above. (Current expectation: zero unknowns — CLI

166	  migration step starts.)
167	- Record `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time as the
168	  pre-refactor baseline. Save in the PR description and in

171	  that Step 2 (conformance suite) and Step 4 (scheduler) add genuine
172	  new test coverage; Step 11's budget accounts for them separately

175	**Verification of refactor**: Step 11 re-runs the fixtures with a
176	structural JSON diff (not byte-equal) that ignores `CapturedAt`
177	formatting variance and any documented additive fields, asserting
178	all other shapes are preserved.

182	### Step 1 — Add interfaces and universal types

184	**Goal**: CONTRACT-004 declarations exist; no harness implements them yet.

186	**Work**:

188	  - Add `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, `ModelDiscoveryHarness`
189	    interface declarations.

199	### Step 2 — Conformance suite and synthetic harness
200	
201	**Goal**: The contract is testable. Every later migration step runs
202	against this suite.
203	
204	**Work**:
205	- New package `internal/harnesses/harnesstest/` (test helper, not
206	  test-only) containing:
207	  - `RunHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.Harness)` — asserts
208	    `Info`, `HealthCheck` (without invoking the binary), and the
209	    `Execute` setup-vs-runtime-error contract.
210	  - `RunQuotaHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.QuotaHarness)` —
211	    asserts `QuotaStatus` returns a valid `QuotaStatus` value (not
212	    an error) when the cache is empty, asserts `QuotaFreshness` is
213	    positive, reads `SupportedLimitIDs()` and asserts every emitted
214	    `Windows[].LimitID` (after driving a `RefreshQuota` against a
215	    cassette or synthetic state) is a member of that set. The suite
216	    asserts the **contract shape** of single-flight semantics —
217	    that concurrent `RefreshQuota` calls return without error and
218	    share a consistent post-state — but does not assert "exactly one
219	    probe ran." Probe-count single-flight is testable on the
220	    synthetic harness (in-memory counter; covered in the synthetic
221	    test); on real harnesses (claude/codex/gemini) it requires
222	    binary-specific or filesystem-specific harnessing and lives in
223	    each harness's own package tests, not the shared suite.
224	  - `RunAccountHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.AccountHarness)` —
225	    analogous, including `AccountFreshness`.
226	  - `RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.ModelDiscoveryHarness)` —
227	    asserts non-empty `DefaultModelSnapshot`, reads
228	    `SupportedAliases()`, asserts `ResolveModelAlias` resolves each
229	    listed alias (positive path) and returns
230	    `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for an out-of-set family (negative
231	    path). Empty `SupportedAliases()` skips the positive-path
232	    check (opencode/pi case).
233	  - `NewSyntheticQuotaHarness(name string, status QuotaStatus, limitIDs []string) QuotaHarness`,
234	    `NewSyntheticAccountHarness(name string, snapshot AccountSnapshot, freshness time.Duration) AccountHarness`,
235	    `NewSyntheticModelDiscoveryHarness(name string, snapshot ModelDiscoverySnapshot, aliases []string) ModelDiscoveryHarness` —
236	    in-memory implementations used by service-level tests to replace
237	    per-harness snapshot construction. Constructors accept the values
238	    each `Supported*` method should return so tests can exercise both
239	    populated and empty contract surfaces.
240	- Add a smoke test that runs the synthetic harnesses against the
241	  conformance suite (proves the suite works before any real harness
242	  consumes it).

265	### Step 4 — Async refresh scheduler
266	
267	**Goal**: A single service-level scheduler consumes
268	`QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`
269	to drive refreshes. Replaces today's per-harness
270	`Refresh*Async`/`refreshClaudeQuotaAsync` helpers.
271	
272	**Work**:
273	- **Introduce a `harnessByName(name string) harnesses.Harness`
274	  method on the service struct** (consulting the registered-harnesses
275	  field) if it does not already exist. This is the seam every later
276	  step uses to fetch the registered harness instance and type-assert
277	  it to the optional sub-interfaces. Logically belongs with the
278	  scheduler; lifting it here means Step 5b can consume it without
279	  introducing it.
280	- New file `service_refresh_scheduler.go`. Owns:
281	  - A debounced ticker per registered `QuotaHarness` keyed by
282	    harness name, firing at `QuotaFreshness()/2` and calling
283	    `RefreshQuota(ctx)`.
284	  - Analogous tickers for `AccountHarness` instances whose
285	    freshness window differs from quota's (e.g. gemini's 7-day
286	    auth).
287	  - Startup refresh kickoff (cache-cold path from
288	    `primary-harness-capability-baseline.md`).
289	  - Tests with a fake clock that prove the scheduler debounces,
290	    respects single-flight (because `RefreshQuota` blocks on the
291	    harness's cache lock), and exits cleanly on context cancel.
292	- **Harness registration mechanism**: at construction time the
293	  scheduler iterates `service.harnessByName(name)` over every name
294	  in the existing harness registry and type-asserts each result to
295	  `QuotaHarness` and `AccountHarness` independently. Pre-migration,
296	  type assertions fail for non-migrated harnesses and the scheduler
297	  registers nothing for them — they continue to use their existing
298	  `Refresh*Async` helpers. Post-migration (Steps 5–8), the
299	  assertions succeed and the migrated harness participates. No
300	  explicit per-harness registration call is needed; the scheduler
301	  picks up new harnesses on its next construction.
302	- The scheduler is the only place that deduplicates probe calls by
303	  cache freshness; `QuotaHarness.RefreshQuota` itself probes
304	  unconditionally per CONTRACT-004. The scheduler skips a tick when
305	  the last-known `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` is within
306	  `QuotaFreshness()` of `now`.
307	- Per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions are not yet deleted — they
308	  stay until their owning harness migrates in Steps 5–8. Each
309	  harness's Step 5e/6e/7e sub-PR deletes its `Refresh*Async` after
310	  confirming the scheduler is driving its refresh cadence.
311	
312	**Size**: 1 PR.

558	## Test Strategy
559	
560	| Layer | Coverage |
561	|-------|----------|
562	| Unit (per harness) | Interface method implementations; routing-preference mapping; cache I/O against synthetic files. |
563	| Conformance suite | Every harness runs Step 2 assertions for its declared interfaces. Suite tests itself against the synthetic harness in Step 2. |
564	| Service-level | JSON-shape preservation against Step 0 fixtures via structural diff in Step 11; routing decisions against synthetic `QuotaHarness` implementations covering every `RoutingPreference` × `QuotaStateValue` combination. |
565	| Integration | Existing `harness_golden_integration_test.go` cassettes continue to pass without modification (cassettes are below the interface boundary). |
566	| Concurrency | The conformance suite asserts `RefreshQuota` and `RefreshAccount` single-flight under concurrent invocation (CONTRACT-004 invariant #7). |
567	| Lint | Step 10 boundary rule runs in CI. |
568	| Regression | Pre-existing test suite within 10% of Step 0 baseline (asserted in Step 11); no individual pre-existing test slower by more than 10%; new conformance + scheduler tests budgeted separately. |
569	
570	## Rollback Strategy
571	
572	Each step's PR is independently revertable.
573	
574	- **Cache file compatibility**: All per-harness cache files use
575	  field-tagged JSON encoding. Renaming the Go type from
576	  `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` to `claudeQuotaSnapshot` does not change the
577	  on-disk JSON shape (verified for each harness in Step 5a/6a/7a
578	  before unexporting). A revert of Step 5e reintroduces the exported
579	  type name without invalidating cached files written under the new
580	  code.
581	- **Per-harness rollback**: Reverting Step 5 (claude) does not require
582	  reverting Steps 6–8 (codex/gemini/opencode/pi) because each
583	  harness's interface implementation is independent. The reverted
584	  harness goes back to its per-harness public surface; the others
585	  remain on the interface.
586	- **Pre-lint coexistence**: Step 10's lint rule lands last
587	  intentionally so that partial-state environments (some harnesses
588	  migrated, some not) compile and test. A revert during this window
589	  produces a working tree; a revert after Step 10 may require
590	  reverting Step 10 alongside.
591	- **Public JSON shape**: Step 11's structural-diff assertion is the
592	  guarantee that any merged state preserves CONTRACT-003. A diff at
593	  Step 11 blocks merge; rollback is a revert of the offending step,
594	  not a forward-fix on a broken contract.
595	
596	## Risks and Mitigations
597	
598	| Risk | Mitigation |
599	|------|------------|
600	| JSON shape regression during projection refactor | Step 0 fixtures pinned before any refactor work; Step 11 asserts structural equality with documented time-field handling. |
601	| Routing semantics drift (boolean `PreferX` → `RoutingPreference` enum) | Step 5 (claude) lands fully and runs the routing test suite before Step 6 (codex) begins. Each subsequent harness re-runs the suite. |
602	| Cache file schema drift when snapshots are renamed | Cache files use field-tagged JSON; type renames don't affect on-disk shape. Each Step 5a/6a/7a includes a load-old-write-new round-trip test before unexporting. |
603	| Conformance suite is too strict and blocks legitimate variation | Conformance covers only CONTRACT-004 contract behavior. Harness-specific tests stay in the harness package. The suite is itself tested in Step 2 against the synthetic harness. |
604	| Lint exemption file accumulates as a backdoor | The exemption list in Step 10 is one line; growing it requires an ADR amendment. |
605	| Refactor stalls partway, leaving mixed surface | Step 10 (lint rule) lands last; partial state is a valid intermediate. Each harness migration's sub-sequence (5e/6e/7e/8) deletes the old surface as an explicit acceptance gate before the next harness starts. |
606	| `claude-tui` work resurfaces during the refactor and bypasses it | ADR-013 is withdrawn (not paused); re-proposal must cite the merged CONTRACT-004. |
607	| Async refresh scheduler is wrong and refreshes too often or too rarely | Step 4's scheduler tests use a fake clock and assert exact tick counts per `QuotaFreshness()` value. Production scheduling defects are caught by `service_status` integration tests measuring actual refresh cadence. |
608	| Step 5 sub-sequence stalls partway (5b lands, 5c does not) | Intermediate states (5a-5d) are valid: claude's new interface methods coexist with the old surface. The old surface only disappears in 5e. |
609	
610	## Acceptance Criteria
611	
612	1. `internal/harnesses/types.go` declares the four interfaces and
613	   universal types per CONTRACT-004.
614	2. Every existing harness implements the interfaces appropriate to it
615	   (verified by the Step 2 conformance suite).
616	3. No `.go` file outside `internal/harnesses/` imports a symbol from
617	   `internal/harnesses/<name>/` except
618	   `internal/serviceimpl/execute_dispatch.go`.
619	4. Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot` types are lowercase.
620	5. Per-harness `*QuotaRoutingDecision` types are deleted.
621	6. Per-harness cache I/O functions are unexported.
622	7. The async refresh scheduler from Step 4 owns all per-harness
623	   refresh cadence; per-harness `Refresh*Async` helpers are deleted.
624	8. CONTRACT-003 JSON fixtures (Step 0) pass structural diff
625	   post-refactor (Step 11).
626	9. Lint rule from Step 10 runs in CI and blocks new forbidden imports.
627	10. PRD, CONTRACT-003, and AGENTS.md cross-references are updated.
628	11. Pre-existing test suite runtime is within 10% of the Step 0
629	    baseline (excluding new packages from Steps 2 and 4); no
630	    individual pre-existing test slows by more than 10%.
631	
632	## Follow-Ups (Out of Scope)
633	
634	- **`claude-tui` re-proposal.** Once this refactor lands, ADR-013
635	  may be re-proposed citing CONTRACT-004. The fork then becomes
636	  additive: a new `internal/harnesses/claude-tui/` package
637	  implementing the same four interfaces, sharing a snapshot type with
638	  `claude` through an `internal/harnesses/anthropic/` neutral
639	  subpackage (since both snapshots are now package-private, sharing
640	  them requires only moving the type into a shared internal
641	  package).
642	
643	  ADR-013's withdrawal note records design constraints for the
644	  re-proposal — prior art surveyed (claude-p, shannon),
645	  transcript-JSONL-as-evidence convergence, hooks-driven transport
646	  via `--settings '<inline-json>'`, pooled long-lived sessions with
647	  `/clear` between turns bounded by the fiz process lifetime, pool
648	  key per `(harness, workdir)` at package or service scope (not on
649	  the Runner struct), startup orphan reaper analogous to the
650	  existing `service_stale_harness_reaper*.go`, hook-conflict
651	  handling against operator `~/.claude/settings.json`, and the
652	  pre-implementation gate that verifies `/clear` semantics against
653	  the installed Claude Code version. Re-proposal still requires
654	  empirical evidence that PTY+hooks-driven Claude moves the
655	  `/usage` window — the structural refactor here does not validate
656	  that premise. CONTRACT-004 and ADR-014 are transport-agnostic and
657	  unaffected by these constraints; they live below the interface.
658	- **`HarnessConfig` registry cleanup.** The current `HarnessConfig`
659	  struct mixes interface-relevant metadata with subprocess-specific
660	  knobs. Splitting these is a separate cleanup; CONTRACT-004 does
661	  not require it.
662	- **Provider-side interface.** HTTP providers (`openrouter`,
663	  `lmstudio`, `omlx`) have their own per-provider exports. A
664	  parallel `ProviderHarness` contract is plausible but not in scope.
665

23		store := runtimesignals.NewStore()

33		require.NoError(t, err)

161		assert.Equal(t, sig.RecentP50Latency, got.RecentP50Latency)
162		require.NotNil(t, got.QuotaResetAt)

497		wg.Add(1)
498		captureCalled := false
499		capture := func() (ClaudeQuotaSnapshot, error) {
500			defer wg.Done()
501			captureCalled = true
502			return ClaudeQuotaSnapshot{
503				CapturedAt:        time.Date(2026, 4, 12, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
504				FiveHourRemaining: 1234,

508				Source:            "pty",
509			}, nil
510		}

512		RefreshClaudeQuotaAsync(capture)
513		wg.Wait()
514	
515		// Poll briefly for the file to land (goroutine writes after capture returns).
516		deadline := time.Now().Add(2 * time.Second)
517		var loaded *ClaudeQuotaSnapshot
518		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
519			if s, ok := ReadClaudeQuotaFrom(path); ok {
520				loaded = s
521				break
522			}
523			time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)

23	printf 'Claude Max\r\n❯ '
24	IFS= read line

26	printf 'Current week (all models)\r\n'
27	sleep 5
28	`), 0o700))
29		cassetteDir := filepath.Join(dir, "cassette")
30	
31		windows, account, err := ReadClaudeQuotaViaPTY(200*time.Millisecond, WithQuotaPTYCommand(script), WithQuotaPTYCassetteDir(cassetteDir))
32		require.Error(t, err)
33		require.Empty(t, windows)
34		require.Nil(t, account)
35		require.Equal(t, ptyquota.StatusError, ptyquota.ErrorStatus(err))
36		require.Contains(t, err.Error(), "timed out")
37		_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(cassetteDir, cassette.ManifestFile))
38		require.True(t, errors.Is(statErr, os.ErrNotExist), "partial usage output should not promote a cassette")

14	func Test_quotaCassetteReplayClaude(t *testing.T) {
15		dir := writeClaudeQuotaCassette(t, fixtureClaudeUsageOutput)
16	
17		windows, account, err := ReadClaudeQuotaFromCassette(dir)
18		require.NoError(t, err)
19		require.NotNil(t, account)
20		require.Equal(t, "Claude Max", account.PlanType)
21		require.Len(t, windows, 4)
22	
23		reader, err := cassette.Open(dir)
24		require.NoError(t, err)
25		require.NotNil(t, reader.Quota())
26		require.Equal(t, "pty", reader.Quota().Source)
27	}
28	
29	func writeClaudeQuotaCassette(t *testing.T, text string) string {
30		t.Helper()
31		dir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "claude-quota")
32		size := session.Size{Rows: 50, Cols: 220}
33		rec, err := cassette.Create(dir, cassette.Manifest{
34			ID:      "claude-quota-replay",
35			Harness: cassette.Harness{Name: "claude"},
36			Command: cassette.Command{
37				Argv:          []string{"claude"},
38				WorkdirPolicy: "test",
39				EnvAllowlist:  []string{"TERM", "LANG", "LC_ALL"},
40				TimeoutMS:     1000,
41			},
42			Terminal: cassette.Terminal{
43				InitialRows: int(size.Rows),
44				InitialCols: int(size.Cols),
45				Locale:      "C.UTF-8",
46				Term:        "xterm-256color",
47				PTYMode:     map[string]any{"outer": "test"},
48				Emulator:    cassette.Emulator{Name: "vt10x", Version: "v0.0.0-20220301184237-5011da428d02"},
49			},
50			Timing: cassette.Timing{ResolutionMS: 50, ClockPolicy: "test", ReplayDefault: cassette.ReplayCollapsed},
51			Provenance: cassette.Provenance{
52				OS:              runtime.GOOS,
53				Arch:            runtime.GOARCH,
54				RecorderVersion: "quota-replay-test",
55			},
56		})
57		require.NoError(t, err)
58	
59		emu := terminal.New(terminal.Size{Rows: int(size.Rows), Cols: int(size.Cols)})
60		chunk := session.OutputChunk{Bytes: []byte(text)}
61		_, err = rec.RecordOutput(chunk)
62		require.NoError(t, err)
63		frame, err := emu.Feed(chunk.Bytes)
64		require.NoError(t, err)
65		_, err = rec.RecordFrame(frame)
66		require.NoError(t, err)
67		windows, account := parseClaudeUsageOutput(text)
68		require.NoError(t, rec.WriteQuota(quotaRecord(windows, map[string]any{"plan_type": accountPlan(account)})))
69		require.NoError(t, rec.RecordFinal(cassette.FinalRecord{FinalText: text, Metadata: map[string]any{"harness": "claude"}}))
70		require.NoError(t, rec.WriteScrubReport(cassette.ScrubReport{
71			Status:                 "clean",
72			Rules:                  []string{"test-fixture"},
73			HitCounts:              map[string]int{},
74			IntentionallyPreserved: []string{"sanitized-quota-fixture"},
75		}))
76		require.NoError(t, rec.Close())
77		return dir
78	}

data.text: Let me read `quota_cache.go` to understand the struct shape for raw JSON writes:

data.output:
1	package claude
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/harnesses"
12		"github.com/easel/fizeau/internal/productinfo"
13	)
14	
15	// claudeQuotaSnapshot captures Claude's current-quota headroom as absolute
16	// token/message counts. It is written to a durable per-user cache by an
17	// asynchronous capture path and read by foreground routing consumers.
18	//
19	// The snapshot is intentionally distinct from the percentage-based
20	// QuotaSignal: foreground routing needs concrete numbers to reason about
21	// 5-hour / weekly headroom without invoking PTY capture inline.
22	type claudeQuotaSnapshot struct {
23		CapturedAt        time.Time               `json:"captured_at"`
24		FiveHourRemaining int                     `json:"five_hour_remaining"`
25		FiveHourLimit     int                     `json:"five_hour_limit"`
26		WeeklyRemaining   int                     `json:"weekly_remaining"`
27		WeeklyLimit       int                     `json:"weekly_limit"`
28		Windows           []harnesses.QuotaWindow `json:"windows,omitempty"`
29		Source            string                  `json:"source"` // e.g. "pty", "heuristic"
30		Account           *harnesses.AccountInfo  `json:"account,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// defaultClaudeQuotaStaleAfter is the default maximum age before a cached
34	// snapshot is considered stale and foreground routing should fall back to
35	// the safe default.
36	const defaultClaudeQuotaStaleAfter = 15 * time.Minute
37	
38	// claudeQuotaCacheEnv lets tests override the cache file path.
39	const claudeQuotaCacheEnv = "FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE"
40	
41	// claudeQuotaCachePath returns the durable location for the Claude quota
42	// cache. It resolves to $XDG_STATE_HOME/<config-dir>/claude-quota.json, or
43	// ~/.local/state/<config-dir>/claude-quota.json when XDG_STATE_HOME is unset.
44	// The FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE env var takes precedence (primarily for
45	// tests).
46	func claudeQuotaCachePath() (string, error) {
47		if path := os.Getenv(claudeQuotaCacheEnv); path != "" {
48			return path, nil
49		}
50		if xdg := os.Getenv("XDG_STATE_HOME"); xdg != "" {
51			return filepath.Join(xdg, productinfo.ConfigDir, "claude-quota.json"), nil
52		}
53		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
54		if err != nil {
55			return "", err
56		}
57		return filepath.Join(home, ".local", "state", productinfo.ConfigDir, "claude-quota.json"), nil
58	}
59	
60	// writeClaudeQuota atomically persists a claudeQuotaSnapshot to the given
61	// path. The parent directory is created if necessary. The file is written
62	// to a sibling .tmp file and renamed into place so readers never observe a
63	// partially-written snapshot. The final file mode is 0600.
64	func writeClaudeQuota(path string, snapshot claudeQuotaSnapshot) error {
65		if path == "" {
66			return fmt.Errorf("claude quota cache path is empty")
67		}
68		if snapshot.CapturedAt.IsZero() {
69			snapshot.CapturedAt = time.Now().UTC()
70		} else {
71			snapshot.CapturedAt = snapshot.CapturedAt.UTC()
72		}
73	
74		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
75			return fmt.Errorf("create claude quota cache dir: %w", err)
76		}
77	
78		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(snapshot, "", "  ")
79		if err != nil {
80			return fmt.Errorf("marshal claude quota snapshot: %w", err)

// TestCollect_Claude_QuotaCache verifies AC3:
// Collect() against a mock claude harness reads the existing quota_cache.go data.
func TestCollect_Claude_QuotaCache(t *testing.T) {
	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
	cachePath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "claude-quota.json")
	t.Setenv("FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE", cachePath)

	snap := claudecache.ClaudeQuotaSnapshot{
		CapturedAt:        time.Now().UTC(),
		FiveHourRemaining: 500,
		FiveHourLimit:     1000,
		WeeklyRemaining:   5000,
		WeeklyLimit:       10000,
		Source:            "test",
	}
	require.NoError(t, claudecache.WriteClaudeQuota(cachePath, snap))

	store := runtimesignals.NewStore()
	sig, err := store.Collect(context.Background(), "claude-subscription", runtimesignals.CollectInput{Type: "claude"})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, runtimesignals.StatusAvailable, sig.Status)
	require.NotNil(t, sig.QuotaRemaining, "QuotaRemaining should reflect FiveHourRemaining")
	assert.Equal(t, 500, *sig.QuotaRemaining)
}

// TestCollect_Claude_Exhausted verifies that a zero 5h window drives StatusExhausted.
func TestCollect_Claude_Exhausted(t *testing.T) {
	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
	cachePath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "claude-quota-exhausted.json")
	t.Setenv("FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE", cachePath)

	snap := claudecache.ClaudeQuotaSnapshot{
		CapturedAt:        time.Now().UTC(),
		FiveHourRemaining: 0,
		FiveHourLimit:     1000,
		WeeklyRemaining:   5000,
		WeeklyLimit:       10000,
		Source:            "test",
	}
	require.NoError(t, claudecache.WriteClaudeQuota(cachePath, snap))

	store := runtimesignals.NewStore()
	sig, err := store.Collect(context.Background(), "claude-subscription", runtimesignals.CollectInput{Type: "claude"})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, runtimesignals.StatusExhausted, sig.Status)
}

// TestCollect_Claude_QuotaCache verifies AC3:
// Collect() against a mock claude harness reads the existing quota_cache.go data.
func TestCollect_Claude_QuotaCache(t *testing.T) {
	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
	cachePath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "claude-quota.json")
	t.Setenv("FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE", cachePath)

	snap := map[string]any{
		"captured_at":         time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
		"five_hour_remaining": 50,
		"five_hour_limit":     100,
		"weekly_remaining":    50,
		"weekly_limit":        100,
		"source":              "test",
		"windows": []any{
			map[string]any{"name": "Current session", "limit_id": "session", "window_minutes": 300, "used_percent": 50.0},
			map[string]any{"name": "Current week", "limit_id": "weekly-all", "window_minutes": 10080, "used_percent": 50.0},
		},
		"account": map[string]any{"plan_type": "Claude Max"},
	}
	data, err := json.Marshal(snap)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(cachePath, data, 0o600))

	store := runtimesignals.NewStore()
	sig, err := store.Collect(context.Background(), "claude-subscription", runtimesignals.CollectInput{Type: "claude"})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, runtimesignals.StatusAvailable, sig.Status)
	require.NotNil(t, sig.QuotaRemaining, "QuotaRemaining should reflect session window remaining")
	assert.Equal(t, 50, *sig.QuotaRemaining)
}

// TestCollect_Claude_Exhausted verifies that a zero 5h window drives StatusExhausted.
func TestCollect_Claude_Exhausted(t *testing.T) {
	tmpDir := t.TempDir()
	cachePath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "claude-quota-exhausted.json")
	t.Setenv("FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE", cachePath)

	snap := map[string]any{
		"captured_at":         time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
		"five_hour_remaining": 0,
		"five_hour_limit":     100,
		"weekly_remaining":    50,
		"weekly_limit":        100,
		"source":              "test",
		"windows": []any{
			map[string]any{"name": "Current session", "limit_id": "session", "window_minutes": 300, "used_percent": 100.0},
			map[string]any{"name": "Current week", "limit_id": "weekly-all", "window_minutes": 10080, "used_percent": 50.0},
		},
		"account": map[string]any{"plan_type": "Claude Max"},
	}
	data, err := json.Marshal(snap)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(cachePath, data, 0o600))

	store := runtimesignals.NewStore()
	sig, err := store.Collect(context.Background(), "claude-subscription", runtimesignals.CollectInput{Type: "claude"})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, runtimesignals.StatusExhausted, sig.Status)
}

626					"power-5": {Type: "test", BaseURL: "http://127.0.0.1:1111/v1", Model: "power-5"},
627					"power-7": {Type: "test", BaseURL: "http://127.0.0.1:2222/v1", Model: "power-7"},
628					"power-9": {Type: "test", BaseURL: "http://127.0.0.1:3333/v1", Model: "power-9"},

696	func TestResolveRouteErrorIncludesCandidatesAndTraceError(t *testing.T) {
697		t.Setenv("GEMINI_API_KEY", "redacted")
698		t.Setenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY", "")
699		t.Setenv("GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI", "")
700		t.Setenv("GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_GCA", "")
701		t.Setenv("GEMINI_CLI_USE_COMPUTE_ADC", "")
702		t.Setenv("CLOUD_SHELL", "")
703	
704		registry := harnesses.NewRegistry()
705		registry.LookPath = func(file string) (string, error) {
706			if file == "gemini" {
707				return "/usr/bin/gemini", nil
708			}
709			return "", os.ErrNotExist
710		}
711		svc := &service{
712			opts:     ServiceOptions{},
713			registry: registry,
714			hub:      newSessionHub(),
715		}
716	
717		dec, err := svc.ResolveRoute(context.Background(), RouteRequest{
718			Model: "minimax/minimax-m2.7",
719		})
720		if err == nil {
721			t.Fatal("ResolveRoute expected no viable candidate error")
722		}
723		if dec == nil {
724			t.Fatal("ResolveRoute error path returned nil decision")
725		}
726		if dec.Harness != "" || dec.Provider != "" || dec.Model != "" {
727			t.Fatalf("error decision selected a candidate: %#v", dec)
728		}
729		if len(dec.Candidates) == 0 {
730			t.Fatal("error decision Candidates is empty")
731		}
732	
733		var noMatch *ErrModelConstraintNoMatch
734		if !errors.As(err, &noMatch) {
735			t.Fatalf("errors.As no-match: %T %v", err, err)
736		}
737		var traced DecisionWithCandidates
738		if !errors.As(err, &traced) {
739			t.Fatalf("errors.As DecisionWithCandidates: %T %v", err, err)
740		}
741		tracedCandidates := traced.RouteCandidates()
742		if len(tracedCandidates) != len(dec.Candidates) {
743			t.Fatalf("traced candidates length=%d, decision candidates length=%d", len(tracedCandidates), len(dec.Candidates))
744		}
745		tracedCandidates[0].Reason = "mutated"
746		if dec.Candidates[0].Reason == "mutated" {
747			t.Fatal("RouteCandidates returned an alias of the decision candidates")
748		}
749	
750		if !strings.Contains(err.Error(), "no matching model") {
751			t.Fatalf("error=%q, want no matching model detail", err.Error())
752		}
753	}

755	func TestRoutingInputsUseClaudeQuotaWindows(t *testing.T) {
756		dir := t.TempDir()
757		cachePath := filepath.Join(dir, "claude-quota.json")
758		t.Setenv("FIZEAU_CLAUDE_QUOTA_CACHE", cachePath)
759	
760		if err := claudeharness.WriteClaudeQuota(cachePath, claudeharness.ClaudeQuotaSnapshot{
761			CapturedAt:        time.Now().UTC(),
762			FiveHourRemaining: 90,
763			FiveHourLimit:     100,
764			WeeklyRemaining:   90,
765			WeeklyLimit:       100,
766			Source:            "pty",
767			Account:           &harnesses.AccountInfo{PlanType: "Claude Max"},
768			Windows: []harnesses.QuotaWindow{
769				{Name: "extra", LimitID: "claude-extra", UsedPercent: 100, State: "exhausted"},
770			},
771		}); err != nil {
772			t.Fatalf("WriteClaudeQuota: %v", err)
773		}
774	
775		registry := harnesses.NewRegistry()
776		registry.LookPath = func(file string) (string, error) {
777			if file == "claude" {
778				return "/usr/bin/claude", nil
779			}
780			return "", os.ErrNotExist
781		}
782		svc := &service{opts: ServiceOptions{}, registry: registry}
783	
784		inputs, _ := svc.buildRoutingInputsWithCatalog(context.Background(), nil, modelsnapshot.RefreshBackground)
785		claudeEntry, ok := findRoutingHarnessEntry(inputs.Harnesses, "claude")
786		if !ok {
787			t.Fatalf("missing claude entry in %#v", inputs.Harnesses)
788		}
789		if claudeEntry.QuotaOK {
790			t.Fatalf("Claude QuotaOK=true, want false for exhausted window: %#v", claudeEntry)
791		}
792		if claudeEntry.SubscriptionOK {
793			t.Fatalf("Claude SubscriptionOK=true, want false for exhausted window: %#v", claudeEntry)
794		}
795		if claudeEntry.QuotaPercentUsed != 100 || claudeEntry.QuotaTrend != routing.QuotaTrendExhausting {
796			t.Fatalf("Claude quota components=%d/%q, want 100/%q", claudeEntry.QuotaPercentUsed, claudeEntry.QuotaTrend, routing.QuotaTrendExhausting)
797		}
798		if !strings.Contains(claudeEntry.QuotaReason, "exhausted claude-extra") {
799			t.Fatalf("Claude QuotaReason=%q, want exhausted claude-extra detail", claudeEntry.QuotaReason)
800		}
801	}

803	func findRoutingHarnessEntry(entries []routing.HarnessEntry, name string) (routing.HarnessEntry, bool) {

995	func TestResolveRouteNearQuotaClaudeDemotesBelowOpenRouter(t *testing.T) {
996		catalog := loadRoutingFixtureCatalog(t, `
997	version: 5
998	generated_at: 2026-04-22T00:00:00Z
999	policies:
1000	  default:
1001	    min_power: 1
1002	    max_power: 10
1003	    allow_local: true
1004	models:
1005	  sonnet-4.6:
1006	    family: claude-sonnet
1007	    status: active
1008	    cost_input_per_m: 3
1009	    cost_output_per_m: 15
1010	    surfaces:
1011	      agent.openai: sonnet-4.6
1012	      claude-code: sonnet-4.6
1013	`)
1014		svc := &service{}
1015	
1016		claude := routing.HarnessEntry{
1017			Name:                "claude",
1018			Surface:             "claude",
1019			CostClass:           "medium",
1020			IsSubscription:      true,
1021			AutoRoutingEligible: true,
1022			Available:           true,
1023			QuotaOK:             true,
1024			QuotaPercentUsed:    92,
1025			QuotaTrend:          routing.QuotaTrendExhausting,
1026			SubscriptionOK:      true,
1027			DefaultModel:        "sonnet-4.6",
1028			ExactPinSupport:     true,
1029			SupportsTools:       true,
1030		}
1031		svc.applySubscriptionRoutingCost(&claude, catalog)
1032	
1033		openrouterProvider := routing.ProviderEntry{
1034			Name:          "openrouter",
1035			BaseURL:       "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
1036			DefaultModel:  "sonnet-4.6",
1037			SupportsTools: true,
1038		}
1039		svc.applyEndpointRoutingCost(&openrouterProvider, ServiceProviderEntry{
1040			Type:    "openrouter",
1041			BaseURL: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
1042			Model:   "sonnet-4.6",
1043		}, catalog)
1044	
1045		in := routing.Inputs{
1046			Harnesses: []routing.HarnessEntry{
1047				claude,
1048				{
1049					Name:                "fiz",
1050					Surface:             "embedded-openai",
1051					CostClass:           "medium",
1052					AutoRoutingEligible: true,
1053					Available:           true,
1054					QuotaOK:             true,
1055					SubscriptionOK:      true,
1056					ExactPinSupport:     true,
1057					SupportsTools:       true,
1058					Providers:           []routing.ProviderEntry{openrouterProvider},
1059				},
1060			},
1061			ObservedSpeedTPS: map[string]float64{
1062				// Neutralize Claude's near-quota score penalty and keep both
1063				// candidates on identical performance evidence so the final base
1064				// scores tie and the cost tiebreak is the deciding dimension.
1065				routing.ProviderModelKey("", "", "sonnet-4.6"):           1900,
1066				routing.ProviderModelKey("openrouter", "", "sonnet-4.6"): 1900,
1067			},
1068		}
1069		dec, err := routing.Resolve(routing.Request{
1070			Policy:             "default",
1071			Model:              "sonnet-4.6",
1072			ProviderPreference: routing.ProviderPreferenceSubscriptionFirst,
1073		}, in)
1074		if err != nil {
1075			t.Fatalf("Resolve: %v", err)
1076		}
1077		if dec.Harness != "fiz" || dec.Provider != "openrouter" {
1078			t.Fatalf("near-quota route selected harness=%q provider=%q, want fiz/openrouter", dec.Harness, dec.Provider)
1079		}
1080		var claudeCandidate, openrouterCandidate routing.Candidate
1081		for _, candidate := range dec.Candidates {
1082			switch {
1083			case candidate.Harness == "claude":
1084				claudeCandidate = candidate
1085			case candidate.Harness == "fiz" && candidate.Provider == "openrouter":
1086				openrouterCandidate = candidate
1087			}
1088		}
1089		if claudeCandidate.Harness == "" || openrouterCandidate.Harness == "" {
1090			t.Fatalf("expected claude and openrouter candidates in trace: %#v", dec.Candidates)
1091		}
1092		if claudeCandidate.Score >= openrouterCandidate.Score {
1093			t.Fatalf("openrouter should outrank near-quota Claude before any cost tiebreak: claude=%.1f openrouter=%.1f", claudeCandidate.Score, openrouterCandidate.Score)
1094		}
1095		if claudeCandidate.CostSource != routing.CostSourceSubscription || !floatNear(claudeCandidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens, 0.009) {
1096			t.Fatalf("claude cost metadata=%#v, want catalog shadow cost 0.009", claudeCandidate)
1097		}
1098		if openrouterCandidate.CostSource != routing.CostSourceCatalog || !floatNear(openrouterCandidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens, 0.009) {
1099			t.Fatalf("openrouter cost metadata=%#v, want catalog cost 0.009", openrouterCandidate)
1100		}
1101		if openrouterCandidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens > claudeCandidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens {
1102			t.Fatalf("openrouter cost %.4f should not exceed claude %.4f", openrouterCandidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens, claudeCandidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens)
1103		}
1104	}

1114	func loadRoutingFixtureCatalog(t *testing.T, contents string) *modelcatalog.Catalog {
1115		t.Helper()
1116		contents = normalizeRoutingFixtureManifest(t, contents)
1117		path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "models.yaml")
1118		requireNoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(contents), 0o600))
1119		catalog, err := modelcatalog.Load(modelcatalog.LoadOptions{ManifestPath: path, RequireExternal: true})
1120		if err != nil {
1121			t.Fatalf("Load fixture catalog: %v", err)
1122		}
1123		return catalog
1124	}
1125	
1126	func normalizeRoutingFixtureManifest(t *testing.T, contents string) string {
1127		t.Helper()
1128		var doc map[string]any
1129		if err := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(contents), &doc); err != nil {
1130			t.Fatalf("parse fixture manifest: %v", err)
1131		}
1132		if version, _ := intFromYAML(doc["version"]); version == 5 {
1133			return contents
1134		}
1135		doc["version"] = 5
1136	
1137		if _, ok := doc["policies"]; !ok {
1138			policies := make(map[string]any)
1139			if profiles, ok := doc["profiles"].(map[string]any); ok {
1140				for name, raw := range profiles {
1141					entry, _ := raw.(map[string]any)
1142					minPower, ok := intFromYAML(entry["min_power"])
1143					if !ok || minPower <= 0 {
1144						minPower = 1
1145					}
1146					maxPower, ok := intFromYAML(entry["max_power"])
1147					if !ok || maxPower <= 0 {
1148						maxPower = 10
1149					}
1150					if _, exists := policies[name]; !exists {
1151						policies[name] = map[string]any{
1152							"min_power":   minPower,
1153							"max_power":   maxPower,
1154							"allow_local": true,
1155						}
1156					}
1157				}
1158			}
1159			if _, ok := policies["default"]; !ok {
1160				policies["default"] = map[string]any{
1161					"min_power":   1,
1162					"max_power":   10,
1163					"allow_local": true,
1164				}
1165			}
1166			doc["policies"] = policies
1167		}
1168		delete(doc, "profiles")
1169		delete(doc, "targets")
1170	
1171		out, err := yaml.Marshal(doc)
1172		if err != nil {
1173			t.Fatalf("marshal fixture manifest: %v", err)
1174		}
1175		return string(out)
1176	}
1177	
1178	func intFromYAML(v any) (int, bool) {
1179		switch n := v.(type) {
1180		case int:
1181			return n, true
1182		case int64:
1183			return int(n), true
1184		case float64:
1185			return int(n), true
1186		default:
1187			return 0, false
1188		}
1189	}

1200	func providerCostsByName(in routing.Inputs, harness string) map[string]routing.ProviderEntry {
1201		out := make(map[string]routing.ProviderEntry)
1202		for _, h := range in.Harnesses {
1203			if h.Name != harness {
1204				continue
1205			}
1206			for _, p := range h.Providers {
1207				out[p.Name] = p
1208			}
1209		}
1210		return out
1211	}

1217			t.Fatalf("provider %q not found in %#v", name, providers)
1218		}
1219		if provider.CostUSDPer1kTokens != wantCost || provider.CostSource != wantSource {
1220			t.Fatalf("provider %q cost=(%v,%q), want (%v,%q)", name, provider.CostUSDPer1kTokens, provider.CostSource, wantCost, wantSource)

1231	func floatNear(got, want float64) bool {
1232		return math.Abs(got-want) < 1e-12
1233	}

1235	// gateFixtureCatalog returns a catalog used by the ContextWindow / RequiresTools
1236	// gate-firing tests below. It declares two concrete models on the agent.openai
1237	// surface: small-ctx-model has a 4096-token context window (and supports
1238	// tools), while no-tools-model has a generous context window but is marked
1239	// no_tools=true so RequiresTools=true requests are rejected against it.
1240	const gateFixtureCatalogYAML = `
1241	version: 5
1242	generated_at: 2026-04-25T00:00:00Z
1243	policies:
1244	  default:
1245	    min_power: 1
1246	    max_power: 10
1247	    allow_local: true
1248	models:
1249	  small-ctx-model:
1250	    family: gate
1251	    status: active
1252	    context_window: 4096
1253	    surfaces: {agent.openai: small-ctx-model}
1254	  no-tools-model:
1255	    family: gate
1256	    status: active
1257	    context_window: 200000
1258	    no_tools: true
1259	    surfaces: {agent.openai: no-tools-model}
1260	`
1261	
1262	func newGateFixtureService(t *testing.T, providerModel string) *service {

1266	
1267		sc := &fakeServiceConfig{
1268			providers: map[string]ServiceProviderEntry{
1269				"local": {Type: "test", BaseURL: "http://127.0.0.1:9999/v1", Model: providerModel},
1270			},
1271			names:       []string{"local"},
1272			defaultName: "local",
1273		}
1274		return publicRouteTraceService(sc)

1277	func findCandidate(t *testing.T, dec *RouteDecision, harness, provider string) RouteCandidate {
1278		t.Helper()
1279		if dec == nil {
1280			t.Fatal("nil decision")
1281		}
1282		for _, c := range dec.Candidates {
1283			if c.Harness == harness && c.Provider == provider {
1284				return c
1285			}
1286		}
1287		t.Fatalf("candidate harness=%q provider=%q not found in %#v", harness, provider, dec.Candidates)
1288		return RouteCandidate{}
1289	}

1291	// TestResolveRoute_FiltersByEstimatedPromptTokens proves the engine's
1292	// context-window gate fires end-to-end: with ContextWindows wired from the
1293	// catalog, a 1M-token prompt against a 4096-token model is marked ineligible
1294	// with FilterReasonContextTooSmall.
1295	func TestResolveRoute_FiltersByEstimatedPromptTokens(t *testing.T) {
1296		svc := newGateFixtureService(t, "small-ctx-model")
1297	
1298		dec, _ := svc.ResolveRoute(context.Background(), RouteRequest{
1299			Harness:               "fiz",
1300			Model:                 "small-ctx-model",
1301			EstimatedPromptTokens: 1_000_000,
1302		})
1303		if dec == nil {
1304			t.Fatal("ResolveRoute returned nil decision")
1305		}
1306		candidate := findCandidate(t, dec, "fiz", "local")
1307		if candidate.Eligible {
1308			t.Fatalf("candidate eligible with 1M tokens against 4096 context: %#v", candidate)
1309		}
1310		if candidate.FilterReason != FilterReasonContextTooSmall {
1311			t.Fatalf("FilterReason=%q, want %q (Reason=%q)", candidate.FilterReason, FilterReasonContextTooSmall, candidate.Reason)
1312		}
1313	}

1337	// TestResolveRoute_NoOpWhenZero proves that an unset EstimatedPromptTokens /
1338	// RequiresTools request does not change which candidates are eligible compared
1339	// to a baseline request — no spurious filtering on the same model that the
1340	// previous two tests reject under stress.
1341	func TestResolveRoute_NoOpWhenZero(t *testing.T) {
1342		smallCtxSvc := newGateFixtureService(t, "small-ctx-model")
1343		noToolsSvc := newGateFixtureService(t, "no-tools-model")
1344	
1345		smallDec, err := smallCtxSvc.ResolveRoute(context.Background(), RouteRequest{
1346			Harness: "fiz",
1347			Model:   "small-ctx-model",
1348		})
1349		if err != nil {
1350			t.Fatalf("ResolveRoute small-ctx-model: %v", err)
1351		}
1352		smallCandidate := findCandidate(t, smallDec, "fiz", "local")
1353		if !smallCandidate.Eligible {
1354			t.Fatalf("small-ctx-model candidate ineligible without EstimatedPromptTokens: %#v", smallCandidate)
1355		}
1356		if smallCandidate.FilterReason != "" {
1357			t.Fatalf("small-ctx-model FilterReason=%q, want empty (no-op gate)", smallCandidate.FilterReason)
1358		}
